metadata.source: Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, China National Textile and Apparel Council (CNTAC), China Leather Industry Association, National Bureau of Statistics

industry_overview.title: China Apparel & Accessories Industry Overview (2026)
industry_overview.summary: China remains the world's largest producer and exporter of apparel and accessories, encompassing garments, footwear, bags, hats, scarves, jewelry, and fashion accessories. In 2026, the industry is projected to exceed ¥5.8 trillion in revenue, driven by domestic consumption upgrades, cross-border e-commerce growth, sustainable fashion trends, and the integration of smart manufacturing. The industry is undergoing a transformation from mass production to flexible, on-demand manufacturing and brand building.

key_policy_initiatives.name: Guidelines for High-Quality Development of Textile and Apparel Industry (2024-2026)
key_measures: Promote digital and intelligent transformation in garment factories
Support the development of domestic fashion brands
Encourage green manufacturing and sustainable materials (recycled fibers, organic cotton)
Establish 20 national-level textile and apparel innovation centers
Facilitate cross-border e-commerce for apparel exports

key_policy_initiatives.name: Action Plan for Leather and Footwear Industry Upgrading
key_measures: Enhance quality and safety standards for leather goods

current_status_2025.automation_rate: 42% (cutting and sewing lines in large enterprises)

upstream.description: Raw materials, fibers, fabrics, trims, and design

subsectors: footwear (sports shoes, leather shoes, casual shoes)

applications: domestic retail (department stores, brand stores, online platforms)
export to global markets (EU, US, Japan, Southeast Asia)

key_hubs.specialization: Women's fashion, wholesale markets, fabric markets
key_hubs.supplier_example: Guangzhou Baiyun World Leather & Footwear Wholesale Center – thousands of suppliers for shoes, bags, accessories
Foshan Shunde Jeans Industry Cluster – home to many denim manufacturers
Dongguan Yikemei Garment Co., Ltd. – specializes in fast fashion womenswear
Huizhou Aokang Shoes Co., Ltd. – one of China's largest footwear manufacturers

strengths: Proximity to Hong Kong for logistics

key_hubs.specialization: Men's suits, formal wear, shirt manufacturing
key_hubs.supplier_example: Youngor Group – leading men's apparel manufacturer, produces for global brands
JNBY (江南布衣) – designer brand with own factories; also many e-commerce sellers
Suzhou Embroidery Research Institute – high-end embroidered products
Aokang Shoes, Red Dragonfly – major footwear brands
Yiwu International Trade Market – world's largest wholesale market for accessories

strengths: Skilled labor in silk and embroidery

key_hubs.supplier_example: Anta, Li-Ning have factories in region; also many OEMs
Anta, Xtep, 361° headquarters; huge OEM cluster
Putian Shoes Industry Cluster – known for both genuine and replica, but many legitimate OEMs
Yudu Jeans Industrial Park – many relocated denim factories

garments.description: Men's, women's, and children's clothing including casual, formal, and sportswear. China produces over 30 billion garments annually.
garments.market_trends: Smart manufacturing (automated cutting, sewing robots); on-demand production; digital printing.

footwear.description: Sports shoes, leather shoes, casual shoes, sandals, slippers. China produces over 15 billion pairs annually.
footwear.market_trends: 3D printing for soles; sustainable materials (recycled plastics, bio-based); smart shoes.

bags_luggage.description: Handbags, backpacks, travel luggage, laptop bags, and fashion bags.
bags_luggage.market_trends: Lightweight materials; smart luggage with charging; vegan leather.

accessories.description: Hats, scarves, gloves, belts, ties, fashion jewelry, hair accessories, and small leather goods.
accessories.market_trends: Sustainable accessories; matching sets; influencer-driven styles.

intimate_apparel.description: Lingerie, shapewear, underwear, sleepwear, and loungewear.
intimate_apparel.market_trends: Seamless knitting; sustainable fabrics; inclusive sizing.

supplier_evaluation_metrics.target: ISO 9001, OEKO-TEX Standard 100, GOTS (organic), BSCI (social compliance), WRAP
Number of production lines, monthly output, machinery (e.g., automated cutting, digital printing)
Social compliance audits (SMETA, BSCI), environmental permits, child labor free
In-house design team, ability to develop samples from sketches
Use of recycled materials, water treatment, energy efficiency

verification_methods: Conduct factory audit (working conditions, production quality)
Order samples for quality, fit, and material testing
Use third-party inspection services (SGS, Bureau Veritas) for pre-shipment inspection
communication_best_practices: Provide detailed tech packs (flat sketches, measurements, materials, trim details)
Discuss sample development timeline and cost
Clarify labeling, packaging, and hanging requirements
Establish quality standards (AQL levels, acceptable defects)
Agree on delivery terms (FOB, CIF, EXW)
sourcing_guide.payment_terms: Typical: 30% deposit, 70% balance before shipment (or against scanned B/L). For sample development, sample cost and courier charges may be prepaid.

faq.question: What are typical MOQs for apparel in China?
faq.answer: MOQ varies: For simple T-shirts, 500-1000 pieces per style/color. For more complex garments (dresses, jackets), 300-600 pieces. For woven shirts, 600-1200 pieces. Some factories accept lower MOQs (100-300) at higher prices. For accessories, MOQ can be 500-2000 pieces depending on item.

faq.question: Do Chinese suppliers provide custom labeling and packaging?
faq.answer: Yes, most suppliers offer OEM services. They can sew your brand labels, hang tags, and provide custom poly bags or boxes. Usually there is a small additional cost.

faq.question: How can I ensure ethical production and avoid sweatshops?
faq.answer: Work with suppliers that have BSCI, SMETA, WRAP, or SA8000 certifications. Conduct your own audits or use third-party audit services. Be cautious of prices that seem too low.

faq.question: What is the best way to get samples made?
faq.answer: Provide a detailed tech pack (sketches, measurements, materials). Some suppliers charge for samples (usually deducted from bulk order). Courier cost is usually paid by buyer. Sample development time is typically 1-3 weeks.

faq.question: Can I source sustainable or organic apparel in China?
faq.answer: Yes, many suppliers now offer GOTS-certified organic cotton, recycled polyester, and eco-friendly dyes. Specify your requirements and ask for certificates.

glossary.AQL: Acceptable Quality Level – sampling standard for inspection.
glossary.BSCI: Business Social Compliance Initiative – social compliance audit.
glossary.CAD: Computer-Aided Design – used for pattern making.
glossary.CMT: Cut, Make, Trim – basic garment production service.
glossary.FOB: Free on Board – seller pays for factory to port shipping.
glossary.GOTS: Global Organic Textile Standard – organic fiber certification.
glossary.OEM: Original Equipment Manufacturer – produces according to buyer's design.
glossary.ODM: Original Design Manufacturer – offers own designs for buyer branding.
glossary.OEKO-TEX: Certification for harmful substances in textiles.
glossary.PP sample: Pre-production sample – approved before mass production.
glossary.Tech pack: Technical package with all product specifications.
glossary.WRAP: Worldwide Responsible Accredited Production – social compliance.

factories.name: Shenzhen Jinguan Textile
factories.specialization: High-end fashion garments, OEM for international brands
